source: Wikipedia’s History of Denmark page says the Code of Jutland was approved at Vordingborg in 1241; this is used here as a clear documented mention of Vordingborg. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/History_of_Denmark)
Wikipedia states that the first recorded mention of Vejle is from 1256. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vejle)
Wikipedia states that Varde is mentioned in written sources from 1107 AD, which is used here as the earliest documented record. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Varde)
Wikipedia states that Vallensbæk is known from at least 1184, when it was mentioned as Wolensbech. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vallensb%C3%A6k)

Wikipedia says Tønder may have been mentioned in the mid-12th century by al-Idrisi as Tu(r)ndira; 1150 is used here as an approximate mid-12th-century year. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/T%C3%B8nder)
Wikipedia lists Thisted’s market-town status as beginning in 1500; this is the clearest dated early record found in a reliable general source. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thisted)
Wikipedia describes Taastrup as a railway town that rose from 1859, so 1859 is used for the modern town/locality in this dataset. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taastrup)
Wikipedia states that the first recorded mention of Svendborg occurred in 1229 in a deed of gift by Valdemar the Victorious. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Svendborg)
Wikipedia says Struer’s growth is tied to the railroad arriving in 1865; this is used here for the modern town record. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Struer,_Denmark)
Wikipedia states that the first known mention of Stenløse was in 1260, recorded as Stenløsæ macla. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C3%98lstykke-Stenl%C3%B8se)

Norwegian Wikipedia states that Smørumnedre was mentioned in King Valdemar’s Census Book in 1231 as a royal estate (kongelev); this is used here as the earliest documented record found. (https://no.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sm%C3%B8rumnedre)

Wikipedia states that Slagelse has existed since the 1000s and that coins were minted there since Cnut the Great’s time; 1000 is used here as an approximate year for that early documented medieval presence. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Slagelse_Municipality)
Wikipedia notes that Skanderborg Castle is first recorded in 1320; this is used here as the earliest clear dated record tied to Skanderborg. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Riberhus)
Wikipedia says the modern town of Silkeborg grew around the paper mill established in 1844, so 1844 is used for the town in its modern sense. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Silkeborg)
Wikipedia says Roskilde was founded in the 980s by Harald Bluetooth; 980 is used here as an approximate year from that source. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roskilde)
Wikipedia lists Rønne as founded in 1327; this is used here as the earliest clear dated record found in a reliable general source. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/R%C3%B8nne)

A Copenhagen Museum historical report says Rødovre is included among the surrounding villages named in Pope Urban III’s letter of 21 October 1186, so 1186 is used here. (https://cphmuseum.kk.dk/sites/default/files/2021-11/Cultural%20historical%20report%20Cat%202%20Metro%20Cityring.pdf)
Trap Danmark states that Ringsted is first mentioned in written sources in 1135, so 1135 is used here. (https://trap.lex.dk/Ringsteds_historie)

Trap Danmark states that Ringkøbing is first mentioned in writing in 1325, though the town probably existed earlier, so 1325 is used here. (https://trap.lex.dk/%C3%98ster_Strandgade_13%2C_Ringk%C3%B8bing)

Wikipedia’s etymology/history section says the oldest forms of Randers appear on coins from the time of Canute the Holy, dated 1080–1086; the earliest year in that documented range, 1080, is used here. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Randers)

Wikipedia’s history section says Odense celebrated its thousandth anniversary in 1988 based on the first mention of the town’s name in a letter dated 18 March 988, so 988 is used here. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Odense)
Wikipedia states that Odder is first mentioned in 1363 as Oddræth, so 1363 is used here. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Odder)
Trap Danmark’s place-name entry says Nykøbing Falster is earliest mentioned in 1231 in copied form Nykoping, so 1231 is used here. (https://trap.lex.dk/Nyk%C3%B8bing_F)
Danish Wikipedia states that Nykøbing on Mors is first mentioned on 13 July 1299, so 1299 is used here. (https://da.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nyk%C3%B8bing_Mors)

Wikipedia notes that Nyborg was first mentioned in 1193 as Nyburg, but the town itself was not mentioned before 1202; because this task asks for the city, 1202 is used here. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nyborg)
Trap Danmark states that Næstved was mentioned in a written source for the first time in 1135, so 1135 is used here. (https://trap.lex.dk/N%C3%A6stveds_historie)
Trap Danmark states that Sundby, the predecessor name of Nørresundby, is first mentioned in 1202, so 1202 is used here. (https://trap.lex.dk/N%C3%B8rresundbys_historie)
Wikipedia states that Nakskov received trade privileges in 1266; this is the earliest clear dated documentary record found for the town, so 1266 is used here. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nakskov)
Wikipedia states that the name Middelfart is first recorded as Mæthælfar in Valdemar’s Census Book in 1231, so 1231 is used here. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Middelfart)
No reliable dated first documented mention for this specific modern town/suburb was found in the sources checked, so the year is left blank. ()
No reliable dated first documented mention for this specific modern town/suburb was found in the sources checked, so the year is left blank. ()
No reliable dated first documented mention for this specific town was found in the sources checked, so the year is left blank. ()
Wikipedia states that Kirke Værløse is mentioned in 1321–23 as Vetherløsa maklæ; using the earliest year of that documented range, 1321 is used here. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/V%C3%A6rl%C3%B8se)
A medieval documents index records Lillerød in 1374, and local historical material likewise gives 1374 as the first mention, so 1374 is used here. (https://tekstnet.dk/books/dipdan/1374/)
VisitDenmark states that the town name Korsør is first mentioned in 1241, so 1241 is used here. (https://www.visitdenmark.dk/danmark/explore/korsoer-faestning-gdk718610)

Trap Danmark says the earliest excavated settlement at Køge dates from the 1280s, while the oldest known town privileges are from 1288; for a single year tied to the earliest documented town status, 1288 is used here. (https://trap.lex.dk/K%C3%B8ges_historie)
The city is described as first mentioned in 1043 under the name Havn on the Copenhagen name-history page. (https://da.wikipedia.org/wiki/K%C3%B8benhavns_navn)
Wikipedia states that the name Kongens Lyngby is first recorded in 1348. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kongens_Lyngby)
Wikipedia lists Kolding as first documented in 1231. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kolding)
No reliable source for an earliest documented mention of Klinteby Frihed was found, so this is left blank. ()
Wikipedia says the Kalundborg area was first settled in 1170, which is used here as the earliest record available on the source page. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kalundborg)
Wikipedia states that Jyllinge is first mentioned in 1171 as Iuleghe. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jyllinge)
No reliable source for an earliest documented mention of Ishøj was found in the checked sources, so this is left blank. ()
No reliable source for an earliest documented mention of Ikast itself was found in the checked sources, so this is left blank. ()
Wikipedia says a farm called Ovre was located here about 1170, which is used as the earliest historical record for Hvidovre. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hvidovre)
No single precise first-documented year was found in the checked reliable sources; they only place Horsens in the 11th or 12th century, so this is left blank. ()
Wikipedia says Hørsholm was founded in connection with Hirschholm Palace and gives 1737 for the residence-town plan. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/H%C3%B8rsholm)
Wikipedia states that Holstebro was first mentioned in a letter from Bishop Thyge of Ribe in 1274. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holstebro)
Wikipedia states that Holbæk is first mentioned in Absalon’s letter of 8 June 1199. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holb%C3%A6k)
A travel-history source says Hobro was first mentioned in historical documents in 1407. (https://biveros.com/hobro-denmark/)

Wikipedia says the oldest known royal charter granting Hjørring market-town status was issued on 31 March 1243, which is used here as the earliest documented mention. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hj%C3%B8rring)
Wikipedia states that the name Hillerød is first mentioned in 1552 as Hylderødz. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hiller%C3%B8d)
Wikipedia says Herning was established at the beginning of the 1790s; 1790 is used here as an approximate year because no exact year is given. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Herning)
Wikipedia states that Helsingør was first mentioned as Hælsingør in King Valdemar’s Liber Census Daniæ from 1231. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Helsing%C3%B8r)
No reliable source for an earliest documented mention of Hedensted itself was found in the checked sources, so this is left blank. ()
Wikipedia says Kongens Hedehus, the origin point of Hedehusene, was built in 1641. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hedehusene)
Wikipedia states that Haslev is mentioned for the first time in 1328 as Haslæ. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haslev)
Wikipedia lists Haderslev as first documented in 1050. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haderslev)

Wikipedia says Grindsted is first mentioned in the 14th century; 1300 is used here as an approximate century-start year because the source gives no exact year. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grindsted)
Wikipedia’s Danish page for Greve Landsby says Greve is first mentioned in 1285. (https://da.wikipedia.org/wiki/Greve_Landsby)
Wikipedia says Grenaa was first mentioned in 1231. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grenaa)
Wikipedia’s Danish page says Glostrup is first attested in the period 1186–1197 as Glostorp; the earliest year in that range, 1186, is used here. (https://da.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glostrup)
Wikipedia says a royal document of 25 August 1756 allowed Classen to call the town Frederiksværk, which is used as the first documented mention. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Frederiksv%C3%A6rk)
Trap Danmark says the name Frederikssund appeared for the first time around 1650. (https://trap.lex.dk/Frederikssunds_historie)
Wikipedia says the settlement, then called Fladstrand, is first mentioned in a letter dated 13 March 1572. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Frederikshavn)

A Danish source on Laurids Thura refers to “Ny Hollænderby uden for Vesterport” as Frederiksberg in 1703, which is used here as the earliest documented mention found for the locality name. (https://da.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laurids_Thura)
Wikipedia says Fredericia was founded in 1650 by Frederik III. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fredericia)
Wikipedia’s Danish page says Farum is first mentioned in Roskilde bishop’s land register from about 1370, so the approximate year 1370 is used. (https://da.wikipedia.org/wiki/Farum)
Wikipedia says Esbjerg was established in 1868 when the harbour project was created, which is used here as the first documented record for the town. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Esbjerg)

Wikipedia’s Danish page says the name Charlottenlund comes from Charlottenlund Slot, which received that name in 1733; this is used as the first documented mention found for the locality name. (https://da.wikipedia.org/wiki/Charlottenlund)
Trap Danmark says the settlement, originally Vester Brønderslev, is first mentioned in 1305. (https://trap.lex.dk/Br%C3%B8nderslevs_historie)

Trap Danmark says Brøndby is first mentioned in 1085; because Brøndbyvester is the western part of the historic Brøndby settlement, 1085 is used here. (https://trap.lex.dk/Middelalderen_i_Br%C3%B8ndby_Kommune)
Trap Danmark says Brøndby Strand is a newer place-name first appearing on a 1936 map. (https://trap.lex.dk/Br%C3%B8ndby_Strand)
Trap Danmark says the place-name Birkerød first appears in a document dated *1307 (preserved in a later copy), so 1307 is used here. (https://trap.lex.dk/Birker%C3%B8d)
Trap Danmark says the oldest original attestation of the place-name Avedøre is from 1257 in the form Augthæhør. (https://trap.lex.dk/Aved%C3%B8re)
Aarhus timeline material and bishopric sources note the first mention of Aarhus in 948 with bishop Reginbrand of Aarhus; 948 is used here. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Timeline_of_Aarhus)
Trap Danmark says the oldest attestation of the name Allerød is from 1260 in the form Alryth. (https://trap.lex.dk/Aller%C3%B8d_Kommune)
Wikipedia says the first mention of Aalborg under the name Alabu or Alabur is on coins from 1040. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aalborg)
Trap Danmark says the place-name Albertslund is first mentioned in 1836, originally as the name of a farm. (https://trap.lex.dk/Albertslund)
Wikipedia’s Danish page says the name appears first in written sources in King Valdemar’s Census Book from 1231. (https://da.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aabenraa)
Wikipedia says Võru was founded on 21 August 1784; this is used as the earliest documented record of the city. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/V%C3%B5ru)
Wikipedia says the first written record of the Viljandi stronghold was in 1154, which is used as the earliest documented mention. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Viljandi)
Wikipedia says Valga first appears in the Riga debt register in 1286, which is used as the earliest documented mention. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Valga%2C_Estonia)

Estonian Wikipedia says Tori Manor is first mentioned in 1528; because a clear earlier source for the settlement itself was not found, this manor record is used as the earliest local documented record. (https://et.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tori)
Wikipedia says Tartu is first mentioned in historical records in 1030, when Yaroslav I the Wise established Yuryev. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tartu)

Wikipedia lists 1154 as Tallinn’s first possible appearance on al-Idrisi’s map; although the first confirmed written record is 1219, the earlier possible mention is used here as the earliest known record. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tallinn)
Wikipedia says the locality of Sillamäggi was first mentioned in 1502, which is used as the earliest documented record for Sillamäe. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sillam%C3%A4e)

Wikipedia says a settlement called Tarvanpea, identified with Rakvere, was first mentioned in 1226; this earlier settlement-name record is used instead of the later 1252 Wesenbergh mention. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rakvere)
Britannica says Pärnu was first mentioned in 1251, which is used here as the earliest documented mention. (https://www.britannica.com/place/Parnu)
Wikipedia lists Narva’s first mention as 1172, which is used as the earliest documented record. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Narva)
Wikipedia lists Maardu’s first mention as 1241, which is used as the earliest documented record of the locality. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maardu)

Wikipedia says the first documentation of the Prince-Bishop’s castle in Kuressaare is from 1381; this is used as the earliest written record tied to the town’s origin. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kuressaare)

Wikipedia explains that villages on the territory of modern Kohtla-Järve are first mentioned in 1241, but the settlement named Kohtla-Järve started to grow in 1924; because the task is for the city itself, 1924 is used. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kohtla-J%C3%A4rve)
Wikipedia says the first written mention of Keila comes from the Danish Census Book in 1241. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Keila)
Wikipedia says Jõhvi was first mentioned as a village in 1241 in the Liber Census Daniae. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/J%C3%B5hvi)
Wikipedia says the first written record of Haapsalu dates to 1279, when the town was chartered. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haapsalu)
Wikipedia states that the chapel parish of Ylöjärvi was founded in 1779, which is the earliest clearly documented record I found for Ylöjärvi. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yl%C3%B6j%C3%A4rvi)
Wikipedia gives Ylivieska’s municipal charter year as 1867; I did not find a clearer earlier first-mention source in the checked English sources. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ylivieska)
Wikipedia gives Vihti’s municipal charter year as 1867; I did not find a clearer earlier first-mention source in the checked English sources. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vihti)

Wikipedia states that Varkaus became a market town in 1929; I did not find a reliable earlier documented first mention for the town itself in the checked English sources. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Varkaus)
Wikipedia states that the first mention of Vantaa is in a document issued by King Magnus IV on 14 September 1331. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vantaa)
Wikipedia states that Vammala was chartered in 1907, which is the earliest clear documented record I found for Vammala itself. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vammala)

Wikipedia gives Valkeakoski’s charter year as 1923; I did not find a clearer earlier first-mention source for the town itself in the checked English sources. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Valkeakoski)
Wikipedia states that Vaasa was founded and granted its charter on 2 October 1606. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vaasa)
Wikipedia states that Uusikaupunki was founded and granted its charter on 19 April 1617. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uusikaupunki)
Wikipedia states that Ulvila was granted its town charter in 1365, which is the earliest clear documented record I found for the town. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ulvila)
Wikipedia states that Tuusula became a separate parish in 1643, which is the earliest clear documented record I found for Tuusula. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tuusula)
Wikipedia states that Pope Gregory IX first mentioned the town of Aboa (Turku) in 1229, and the city uses that as its first historical record. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turku)
Wikipedia states that Tornio received its charter and was officially founded on 12 May 1621. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tornio)
Wikipedia’s Messukylä article states that Tampere was mentioned as early as 1666, before it was later founded as a town. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Messukyl%C3%A4)
Wikipedia gives Siilinjärvi’s charter year as 1925; I did not find a clearer earlier first-mention source in the checked English sources. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Siilinj%C3%A4rvi)
Wikipedia gives Sipoo/Sibbo’s charter year as 1425, which is the earliest clear documented record I found in the checked English sources. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sipoo)

Wikipedia gives Seinäjoki’s charter year as 1868; the history text mentions earlier settlement, but it is unsourced on the page, so I used the sourced infobox date. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sein%C3%A4joki)
Wikipedia states that the city of Savonlinna was founded in 1639. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Savonlinna)

Wikipedia’s Halikko article states that the name Halikko, the historical core area of present-day Salo, is first mentioned in 1313; this is used as an approximate earliest documented record for the present city area. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halikko)
Wikipedia states that Rovaniemi is first mentioned by name in official documents in 1453. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rovaniemi)
Wikipedia states that Riihimäki was first mentioned in 1495 as Höffinga. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyvink%C3%A4%C3%A4)
Wikipedia states that Rauma was granted town privileges on 17 April 1442. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rauma%2C_Finland)

Wikipedia states that Raseborg as a municipal name is modern, but Raseborg Castle was first mentioned in 1378 as Rasaborge; this is used as the earliest documented record for the place-name. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raseborg)
The official City of Raahe history page states that the town was founded in 1649 under the name Salo. (https://raahe.fi/en/information-about-raahe/history-raahe)
Wikipedia states that Pori was established on 8 March 1558 by Duke John of Finland. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pori)
Finnish Wikipedia says the name Pirkkala is first mentioned in a document dated 9 May 1374. (https://fi.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pirkkala)

A historical overview states the parish was soon renamed Pieksämä (Pieksämäki) in 1577, which is the earliest clear documentary mention I found for the place name. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mikkelin_maalaiskunta)
Wikipedia states that Pedersöre was first mentioned in 1348 in a trade statute issued by King Magnus Eriksson. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peders%C3%B6re)
Finnish Wikipedia says the medieval parish of Parainen/Pargas is first mentioned in historical sources in 1329. (https://fi.wikipedia.org/wiki/Parainen)
The City of Paimio says the earliest preserved document mentioning Paimio is from 1325. (https://www.paimio.fi/kaupunki-ja-hallinto/tietoa-paimiosta/paimion-historia/)
Wikipedia's history section notes that in 1375 the Novgorodians built a defensive castle at Oulu, which is the earliest clear documentary reference on the page. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oulu)
Wikipedia states that Orimattila as a village was first mentioned in 1561 as Orihmattila. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Orimattila)
Wikipedia says the earliest documentary mention of Nurmijärvi dates to 1488, when the village of Uotila was mentioned. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nurmij%C3%A4rvi)

Finnish Wikipedia says the name appears on maps in 1758–1763 as Nöjkäng; I use 1758 as the earliest attested year and note that this is a map-form of the older Swedish name. (https://fi.wikipedia.org/wiki/N%C3%B6ykki%C3%B6)

The City of Nokia says the name Nokia is first mentioned in written sources in connection with Nokia Manor in 1505. (https://www.nokiankaupunki.fi/kaupunki-ja-hallinto/tutustu-nokiaan/nokian-kaupunki-ennen-ja-nyt/)

The Finnish Genealogical Society page says the parish, then called Pidisjärvi, was established as a prayerhouse community in 1682; I use that earliest documented predecessor mention for present-day Nivala. (https://hiski.genealogia.fi/seurakunnat/srk?CMD=SRK&ID=355&LANG=FI&TYPE=HTML)
Wikipedia says the village of Nastola was first mentioned in 1478, when the first church in the area was built there. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nastola)
Wikipedia says Närpes can be dated back to 1331, when Klas Bengtsson in Nærpes pawned goods to Bishop Bengt in Turku. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/N%C3%A4rpes)
Wikipedia says Naantali was founded around the Brigittine convent and that the town charter was signed in 1443. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Naantali)
Wikipedia says that in 1539 there were four farms in Muurame, which is the earliest dated documentary reference I found for the settlement. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Muurame)

Finnish Wikipedia says the Savilahti mother parish in the Mikkeli area is first mentioned in 1329; I use that earliest documented record for the city area, even though the name Mikkeli itself appears later. (https://fi.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uskonto_Mikkeliss%C3%A4)
Finnish Wikipedia says Masku is first mentioned in 1232 in a letter by Pope Gregory IX. (https://fi.wikipedia.org/wiki/Masku)
Wikipedia says Mäntsälä is considered to have been founded in 1585, when the community's first church was built. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/M%C3%A4nts%C3%A4l%C3%A4)
Wikipedia says Loviisa was founded in 1745 as a border fortress against Russia. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Loviisa)
Finnish Wikipedia says Loimaa is first mentioned as a parish in 1420. (https://fi.wikipedia.org/wiki/Loimaan_kunta)
Finnish Wikipedia says the parish of Lohja is first mentioned in written sources in 1323. (https://fi.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lohja)

The Finnish parish list gives Liperi's earliest dated record as 1630 for its predecessor parish history, which is the earliest clear documentary year I found. (https://fi.wikipedia.org/wiki/Luettelo_Suomen_kirkkopit%C3%A4jist%C3%A4)
The Municipality of Liminka says Liminka is first mentioned in a written source in 1477. (https://www.liminka.fi/kulttuuri-ja-vapaa-aika/kulttuuri/)

Wikipedia says a surviving document from 1331 mentions Lieto's parish priest Pietari for the first time, and that year is treated as the founding year of the parish and church. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lieto)

Wikipedia says the predecessor municipality Pielisjärvi, which later formed modern Lieksa, was first mentioned in 1618 as Pelis Järffwi Pogost; I use that earlier documented record for the present-day municipality area. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pielisj%C3%A4rvi)

FamilySearch’s Lempäälä Parish page says the first written account of the parish of Lempäälä is from 1430. (https://www.familysearch.org/en/wiki/Lemp%C3%A4%C3%A4l%C3%A4_Parish%2C_H%C3%A4me%2C_Finland_Genealogy)
Wikipedia states that Laukaa gained its first subordinate church in 1593, which is used here as the earliest documented mention in the source found. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laukaa)
I could not verify a reliable exact first documented mention year for Lapua from the sources I found, so the year is left blank. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lapua)
Wikipedia’s Lappee history says Lappee was first mentioned as Lappawesi in 1415; this is used for the earlier core settlement behind Lappeenranta. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lappee)
Wikipedia says Lahti was first mentioned in documents in 1445. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lahti)
I could not verify a reliable exact first documented mention year specifically for Laaksolahti from the sources I found, so the year is left blank. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Espoo)
A local history source says the first mention of Kirkkonummi is in a Latin document dated 21 March 1330. (https://mclad.iki.fi/kirkkonummi/index.php?lang=en&page=2)

The best source I found says the first church in Kuusamo was built in 1695 and that by the late 1600s the area around Kuusamojärvi was known as Kuusamo; 1695 is used here as the earliest firm year in that source. (https://kids.kiddle.co/Kuusamo)
I could not verify a reliable exact first documented mention year for Kurikka from the sources I found, so the year is left blank. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kurikka)
Wikipedia says a parish church was established in Kuopionniemi in the 1550s; because the source gives only a decade, 1550 is used as an approximation. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kuopio)

Wikipedia says the village of Kouvola has been inhabited since the Middle Ages, but I did not find a reliable exact first documented mention year, so the year is left blank. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kouvola)

Wikipedia says the declaration establishing the city on the islands was issued on 16 April 1878, which is used here as the first documented record for Kotka as a city. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kotka)
Wikipedia says the parish was first mentioned as Mustasaari parish in 1348; this is used for Korsholm. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Korsholm)

Britannica says Helsingborg was first mentioned as a town in 1085, which is used here as the first documented mention. (https://www.britannica.com/place/Helsingborg)

A local history guide states that the Hässleholm estate is first mentioned in historical documents in 1539, and this is used as the earliest documented record behind the later town name. (https://www.kupi.com/en-ae/explore/sweden/hassleholm/history)

Official regional material states that Härnösand was founded in 1585 by King Johan III, which is used here as the first documented city record. (https://www.lansstyrelsen.se/download/18.8cd5a1b19362fb4fc22fc0/1732538526141/Bilaga%202.pdf)

I did not find a reliable source giving a specific first documented mention year for Haninge as a locality rather than the later municipality, so the year is left blank. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haninge_Municipality)

Stockholms läns museum says Handen station opened with the Nynäs line in 1901 and a locality then grew around it, so 1901 is used for the modern locality. (https://stockholmslansmuseum.se/upptack-historien/handen/)
Wikipedia states that Halmstad received its first city charter in 1307, which is used here as the earliest documented city record. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halmstad)

Wikipedia says the first smithy in Hallstahammar was built in 1628, which is the earliest clear dated record I found tied directly to the locality's development. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hallstahammar)

Gustavsberg's official history says the name and history go back to a brickworks from 1640, so 1640 is used as the earliest documented record for the place-name. (https://www.gustavsberg.com/fileadmin/uploads/Brochures/English/ENG_WEB.pdf)
Wikipedia states that Gothenburg was founded by royal charter in 1621, which is used here as the first documented mention of the city in its present form. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gothenburg)
Official Gislaved municipal history says Gislaved is first mentioned in 1434. (https://www.gislaved.se/kommun-och-politik/kommunfakta/historia)
Wikipedia states Gävle is first mentioned as a town in official history books in 1413. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/G%C3%A4vle)
Finspång municipal history refers to the 'fastebrevet från 1435', used here as the earliest documented mention. (https://finspang.se/kommunochpolitik/kommunfakta/historia-och-kommunvapen)
The earliest surviving written document relating to the Great Copper Mountain in Falun is from 1288, used here as the earliest documented record for Falun. (https://whc.unesco.org/document/151569)
Wikipedia says Falköping was first spoken of in the Icelandic Rimbegla around 1100; 1100 is used as an approximate year. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Falk%C3%B6ping)
Wikipedia's Falkenberg fort article says Falkenberg was first mentioned in 1298. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Falkenberg_%28fort%29)

Ekomuseum says Fagersta's roots trace back to the 1400s; 1400 is used as an approximate first-record year because no exact year is given in the source snippet. (https://ekomuseum.se/en/besoksmalen/fagersta-brukssamhalle-bruksmuseum/)
Wikipedia says a map from 1717 shows the original Eslöv village, which is used here as the earliest documented record found. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Esl%C3%B6v)
Wikipedia says Eskilstuna's first city privileges were granted in 1659; this is the clearest dated documented record found for the city as Eskilstuna. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eskilstuna)
Wikipedia says the city itself did not emerge until about 1250; 1250 is used as the earliest documented city date. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Enk%C3%B6ping)
Wikipedia states Eksjö is first mentioned on 22 April 1345 under the name 'Ekesiö'. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eksj%C3%B6)

A runic source summary says the name that became Ekerö appears on a rune stone and another scholarly summary places that evidence in the mid-11th century; 1050 is used as an approximate year. (https://swedenbybike.com/en/day-rides/medieval-churches-at-ekero/)

No reliable first documented mention of the locality Danderyd was found in the sources checked; the municipal history page discusses prehistory but does not give a first written mention. (https://www.danderyd.se/kommun-och-politik/fakta-danderyds-kommun/danderyds-historia/)

No reliable first documented mention of the modern locality Bunkeflostrand was found; available sources describe it as a later suburb built from older Bunkeflo/Vintrie/Naffentorp strand areas. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bunkeflostrand)
Borlänge municipality says the oldest known record of Borlänge is a deed issued on 13 October 1390. (https://www.borlange.se/kommun-och-politik/kommunfakta/historia)
Wikipedia says Borås received its privileges in 1621, which is used here as the first documented city record. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bor%C3%A5s)
Wikipedia says the first written recording of Bollnäs is from 1312, when it was written as Baldenaes. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bolln%C3%A4s)

I did not find a reliable source giving the first written mention of the locality Bjuv itself; readily available sources mainly confirm the medieval church and later municipal history. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bjuv_Church)

An archaeological report snippet says Steneby village in Björlanda is first mentioned in written sources in 1399, used here as the earliest documented mention found for Björlanda. (https://samlingar.goteborgsstadsmuseum.se/carlotta/web/image/blob/1293981/Arkeologisk%2Brapport%2B1975_01.pdf)

I could not verify a reliable first documented mention of Bjärred itself; Lomma municipal history only says Bjärred developed as a seaside resort in the late 1800s/around 1900. (https://lomma.se/download/18.eda591e194a7c909a5375d/1738244882794/%C3%85tg%C3%A4rdsprogram%20f%C3%B6r%20byarna.pdf)
Wikipedia says Billdal was a village in the 1500s; because the source gives only the century, the approximate year 1500 is used here. (https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/Billdal)
Wikipedia states that Bålsta was originally a village in Yttergran parish and is first mentioned in 1316 as 'in Bardestum'. (https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/B%C3%A5lsta)
Wikipedia says the place-name Avesta is first attested in 1303 as 'Aghastadhum', which is used as the earliest documented record. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avesta_%28locality%29)

I could not verify a reliable first documented mention of Åstorp itself from a strong source; available results mainly concern Björnekulla or later municipal history. (https://astorp.se/download/18.355f08901715dd0ef335591/1586263891774/Arkivbeskrivning.pdf)

Arvika municipality says Håkon Håkonsson's saga mentions 'a district called Arvika' in 1225, which is used as the earliest documented record. (https://www.arvika.se/kommunochpolitik/arkivlokalhistoria/historia.2497.html)
Wikipedia says Arlöv is first mentioned in the mid-1100s as 'Arleue'; because the source gives only mid-century, the approximate year 1150 is used here. (https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arl%C3%B6v)

Arboga municipality states that the name Arboga appears in writing for the first time in 1286, which is used here. (https://arboga.se/download/18.42ab9909182c3f0764d111/1661148428650/Arboga%20BOA%20total%20%281%29.pdf)

Ängelholm's official history page says the town received its town privileges in 1516, which is the earliest documented record I found for Ängelholm itself. (https://www.engelholm.se/kommun-och-politik/kommunfakta/angelholms-historia.html)
Wikipedia's Åmåls historia page says Åmål is first known from a 1312 letter, where it appears as 'Ömordh'. (https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C3%85m%C3%A5ls_historia)
Wikipedia states that Älta gård, which gave Älta its name, is mentioned in writing in a deed from 1397, which is used here for the locality. (https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/Erstavik)

I could not verify a reliable first documented mention of Älmhult itself from a strong source; the official municipal page I found documents Älmhult as a köping in 1901 rather than the earliest name attestation. (https://www.almhult.se/kommunpolitik/faktaomalmhult/dethararalmhult.621.html)
Wikipedia says the place-name Alingsås is attested from 1357, which is used as the earliest documented record. (https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alings%C3%A5s)

Wikipedia says a station was built there in 1901 and signposted 'Åkers Berga', from which the present name Åkersberga developed; I use 1901 for the modern locality. (https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C3%85kersberga)

Kristianstad municipality says Åhus is first mentioned in written sources in 1149, when the area was granted to Archbishop Eskil of Lund. (https://www.kristianstad.se/upplevaochgora/arkivochslaktforskning/lokalhistoria/ahus.5369.html)

I could not verify a reliable first documented mention of Onsala itself from a strong source; results found were about later buildings or general local history, not a clear first attestation of the locality. (https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/Onsala)

I could not verify a reliable first documented mention of Alby itself from a strong source; available sources mainly describe modern suburb development or nearby estates rather than a clear first attestation of Alby. (https://www.botkyrka.se/download/18.1fbbaae6170bff9469deb34/1584093803427/Alby%20stadsbyggnadsid%C3%A9.pdf)
